Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log for Procure-to-Pay (P2P) process. Standard P2P flow: Create Purchase Requisition (PR) -> Approve PR -> Issue Purchase Order (PO) -> Receive Goods -> Vendor Invoice Received -> Match Invoice to PO -> Release Payment -> Close Case. Sometimes there can be variations like goods receipt before invoice, etc. But key is that payment should not happen before goods receipt and invoice matching. Also approvals should happen before PO issuance. Let's examine each case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 1001: Sequence: Create PR, Approve PR, Issue PO, Receive Goods, Invoice Received, Match Invoice, Release Payment, Close. That's correct order. No anomaly.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002: Sequence: Create PR, Issue PO, Vendor Invoice Received, Release Payment, Approve PR, Receive Goods, Match Invoice, Close. Here, Issue PO occurs before Approve PR (approval after PO is unusual; normally PR must be approved before PO can be issued). Also, Vendor Invoice Received and Release Payment happen before Approve PR, Receive Goods, and Match Invoice. Payment before goods receipt and matching is a major anomaly. This could indicate fraudulent or erroneous payment. Also approval after payment is problematic. So case 1002 has multiple anomalies.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003: Sequence: Create PR, Approve PR, Issue PO, Release Payment, Invoice Received, Receive Goods, Match Invoice, Close. Here, Release Payment occurs before Invoice Received and before Receive Goods. That's paying before receiving goods and before even receiving invoice. That is highly unusual and risky. Payment should only be made after goods receipt and invoice matching. Also, after payment, they later receive invoice and goods, then match. So anomaly: premature payment.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004: Sequence: Create PR, Approve PR, Receive Goods, Issue PO, Vendor Invoice Received, Match Invoice, Release Payment, Close. Here, Receive Goods occurs before Issue PO. That's unusual because normally you issue PO first, then supplier delivers goods. Receiving goods without a PO is problematic (could be unordered goods). Then they issue PO after goods receipt. Also, Invoice received after that, then match and payment. So anomaly: goods receipt before PO issuance.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus anomalies in cases 1002, 1003, 1004. Need to explain why problematic. Let's provide answer.
